title: Read capability and preference signals
summary: US-004 is fully implemented in src/input/signals.ts with a comprehensive test suite in tests/signals.test.ts. The readSignals() function reads all required capability and preference hints, all values are optional with null fallbacks, and nothing is persisted to storage. The implementation was delivered as part of the US-001 scaffold.

file_map.src/input/signals.ts: BrowserSignals interface + readSignals() — 55 lines, fully implements all 5 acceptance criteria
file_map.tests/signals.test.ts: 12 test cases under 'US-004' describe block (lines 83-194) covering all signals and fallbacks

acceptance_criteria_mapping.implementation: signals.ts:24-29 — matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark|light)') with null fallback
tests: returns dark when prefers-color-scheme is dark
returns light when prefers-color-scheme is light
returns null prefersColorScheme when matchMedia is unavailable

acceptance_criteria_mapping.implementation: signals.ts:30 — matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches with null fallback
tests: returns true when prefers-reduced-motion is reduce
returns false when prefers-reduced-motion is not set
returns null prefersReducedMotion when matchMedia is unavailable

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Read touch capability from available browser APIs
acceptance_criteria_mapping.implementation: signals.ts:37-41 — checks ontouchstart in window || navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0
tests: returns true for touchCapable when ontouchstart exists
returns true for touchCapable when maxTouchPoints > 0
returns false for touchCapable when no touch APIs available

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: All values stay in memory only
acceptance_criteria_mapping.implementation: No localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookie access anywhere in signals.ts
tests: does not access localStorage or sessionStorage (US-003 block)

verification_steps.expected: TypeScript compilation and Vite build succeed with no errors

downstream_consumers.usage: BrowserSignals fed into SeedInputs for deterministic session hash
BrowserSignals used in MappingInputs — DPR/hardwareConcurrency→density, prefersReducedMotion→motionAmplitude
readSignals() called at startup after geo fetch, result passed to session seed init

implementation_plan.action: Run the US-004 test suite to confirm all tests pass
Run full build to confirm no TypeScript or bundling errors
Mark ticket as complete if both pass
